content: I am implementing NVIDIA support for the OPI DPU Operator. I want to keep the reconciliation loop vendor-neutral while allowing vendor-specific implementations. Suggest a Kubernetes Operator architecture that follows the Adapter Pattern and can be extended to support additional vendors in the future.

A suitable architecture is to introduce a VendorAdapter interface responsible for translating a generic OPI DPU specification into a vendor-specific Custom Resource. The reconciler remains vendor-neutral and delegates translation to the appropriate adapter selected from a VendorRegistry. The registry maps vendor names to their adapter implementations. This keeps the reconciliation loop independent of vendor-specific logic while allowing new vendors to be added by implementing the same interface.
How should the reconciliation loop work using this VendorRegistry and VendorAdapter design?

The reconciliation loop first retrieves the OPI DPU Custom Resource and extracts its specification. It reads the vendor field and looks up the corresponding adapter from the VendorRegistry. The selected adapter translates the vendor-neutral specification into a vendor-specific resource. The reconciler establishes an OwnerReference, applies the translated resource using Server-Side Apply, watches the child resource for status updates, and synchronizes the resulting status back to the original OPI DPU resource.
Generate a Mermaid sequence diagram representing this reconciliation workflow.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant API as Kubernetes API Server
    participant OPI as VendorDPFAdapterReconciler
    participant Registry as VendorRegistry
    participant Adapter as NvidiaTranslator
    participant DPF as NVIDIA DPF Operator

    User->>API: Create OPI Dpu CR
    API-->>OPI: Reconcile Event
    OPI->>Registry: Lookup adapter(vendor)
    Registry-->>OPI: NvidiaTranslator
    OPI->>Adapter: Translate(OPI Spec)
    Adapter-->>OPI: DpfDeployment CR
    OPI->>API: Server-Side Apply
    OPI->>API: Set OwnerReference
    API-->>DPF: Watch DpfDeployment
    DPF->>API: Update Status
    API-->>OPI: Status Update Event
    OPI->>API: Update OPI Dpu Status
```
Why is this Vendor Adapter architecture better than implementing NVIDIA-specific logic directly inside the reconciler?

Separating vendor-specific translation behind a VendorAdapter interface keeps the reconciler focused only on reconciliation. This improves maintainability, reduces coupling, and follows the Single Responsibility and Open/Closed principles. Adding support for another vendor such as AMD only requires implementing a new adapter and registering it in the VendorRegistry without modifying the reconciliation loop. The design is also easier to test because adapters can be independently validated.
Generate a foundational Go implementation demonstrating this architecture.

The generated implementation includes a VendorAdapter interface exposing Translate() and OwnedGVK() methods, a VendorRegistry for runtime adapter selection, a VendorDPFAdapterReconciler implementing the Kubernetes reconciliation loop, an NvidiaTranslator that converts OPI DPU resources into NVIDIA DpfDeployment resources, Server-Side Apply for declarative updates, OwnerReference management for resource lifecycle, status synchronization from child resources back to the OPI DPU resource, and dynamic controller watches for registered vendor resources. The implementation serves as a production-oriented foundation that can be extended by registering additional vendor adapters.
